updates.tweet_text:
0 Volatility, don't trade until we get a clear injection of it. 7590 was support given yesterday and #ES_F glued here since 4pm ystd after a nice Failed Breakdown that hit 7611 last eve

ES is very tired/weak now. 7600 must reclaim to see 7611, 7621+

7570 fails, dip 7545-50 https://t.co/ye9cVMolO9
updates.notes: 7590, 7611, 7621 are morning plan levels; 7600 is a reclaim condition observation; 7570/7545-50 are conditional dip references, not explicitly named as new actionable levels.

updates.tweet_text: @TradeLearner11 @justjay_s I don't think that way! During the session I just react to triggers. The structure looks extremely bearish to me though but again thats irrelevant to my trading. If we trigger long I long
updates.notes: Reply tweet expressing trading philosophy and market opinion; no actionable price levels named.

updates.tweet_text: @fiveinchhammer @TradeLearner11 @justjay_s Yes many people take my comments and read them as "predictions", rather than actually focusing on the triggers and the plan. Most beginners can't seperate comments on structure/talking about markets with actual execution of a plan
updates.notes: Meta-commentary about how followers misread the trader's posts. No prices mentioned, no actionable levels named.

updates.tweet_text: @anchovy_paste @TradeLearner11 @justjay_s That's just because its very rarely bearish since we are in a strong bull market!
updates.notes: Conversational reply about market bias; no price levels named or actionable instructions given.

updates.tweet_text: @Bluepit_80 @anchovy_paste @TradeLearner11 @justjay_s The macro structure is very bullish (there is a huge bull flag I posted in my Saturday chart that will very easily give us another 500-1000 points), but we are at flag resistance now and have abit of a lopsided triangle forming that probably needs to break down. Again, no
updates.notes: No specific actionable price levels named; only general macro structure commentary about bull flag and triangle pattern without any explicit price triggers or targets.

updates.tweet_text: @badfutures40 I find lately they've been quite muted! At least compared to historically, almost a non-event recently. But also the technicals are the technicals and same setups apply FOMC or not
updates.notes: Pure conversational reply about FOMC volatility being muted historically; no price levels named or actionable setups mentioned.

updates.tweet_text:
Total chop as we await FOMC. Don't trade noise. As posted, #ES_F is very tired and weak now. In order for bulls to rally today (to 7611, 7621+), 7600 must reclaim. We just backtested and rejected to 7590 which remains a noisy magnet.

7571 fails we can finally sell to 7545-50 https://t.co/lzMqOSa6eC
updates.notes: All prices (7611, 7621, 7600, 7590, 7571, 7545-50) are either morning-plan levels or observations/conditionals referencing existing levels. No genuinely new actionable level introduced.

updates.tweet_text:
Finally a little burst of volatility but would be cautious as its noise until FOMC. As posted all morning #ES_F is tired and weak today as it digests the recent rally and sets up the next one. 

Bulls must recover 7578 now with force to see 7590 magnet again. 7545-50 next down https://t.co/yt2k6Z00kC
updates.notes: 7578 is a new actionable bull reclaim trigger ('must recover with force'); 7545-50 midpoint ~7547.5 is already in morning plan, so bear side yields no new level.

updates.tweet_text:
Nice little pre-FOMC move in #ES_F and it was thanks to the burst of volatility we had. Volatility means we can stop sitting on hands and trade. I posted at 11:01AM 7578 must recover to see 7590 magnet. We recovered 7578, just hit 7590

7600, 7611 if bulls want more on this https://t.co/gZy6aFoRTT
updates.notes: 7600 and 7611 appear but 7611 is a morning plan level; 7600 is listed without naming language—no 'key','added','watching', etc. Reaffirmation/directional list only.

updates.tweet_text:
Initial FOMC reaction in the books in #ES_F. We got a clean pre-FOMC long. The 7578 reclaim was long trigger posted, with 7590 target. We hit 7590 before FOMC and dipped, now flushing again.

7545-50, 7525, 7511 below. 7578 must reclaim now to rally back to 7590+ again. https://t.co/jfsyAbXYbi
updates.notes: 7578 is a reaffirmation of a posted trigger; 7545-50/7525/7511 are observations/directional list of existing levels; 7590 is morning plan level. No new actionable levels.

updates.tweet_text:

updates.tweet_text:

Plan: Ride runner. 7535=support. 7558, 7570, 7590 next. 7509/02  below 35 https://t.co/rxWI6XQp7j
